text:
Let the number of students good at both math and English be \( x \).

Total students = 20  
Good at math only = 5  
Good at English only = 8  
Good at both = \( x \)

So,
\[
5 \text{ (math only)} + 8 \text{ (English only)} + x \text{ (both)} = 20
\]
\[
13 + x = 20
\]
\[
x = 7
\]

Number of students good at math = Good at math only + Good at both  
= \( 5 + 7 = 12 \)

#### 12
